mnemonics: For Module 5, think of the '5' as a 'hand' grabbing the right resources: Official docs are your thumb (most important), RealPython is your index finger (points you forward), and Stack Overflow is your middle finger (for when you're stuck and frustrated).
keyPoints: The official Python documentation is the primary, most reliable source.
Platforms like Real Python and Stack Overflow provide practical, community-vetted examples and solutions.
Interactive platforms like DataCamp and Kaggle Learn offer hands-on, structured learning paths specifically for data science.
Books and video courses provide deep, conceptual understanding for different learning styles.
The best resource depends on your specific learning goal (syntax, library, problem-solving).
commonMistakes: Relying solely on a single resource type (e.g., only videos) instead of a blended approach for comprehensive learning.
Getting stuck in 'tutorial hell'—consuming content without applying it to personal projects.
Using outdated blog posts or tutorials that reference deprecated code or old library versions, leading to errors.
